title: Lesson 20: Mini Project — Build a Login & Signup Page

content.explanation:
🚀 You Are Building Real Web Apps Now

Congratulations — you now understand forms, inputs, labels, and validation.

That means you are ready to build something real: a login and signup interface.

🌐 What You Are Building

  • A signup form for new users
  • A login form for existing users
  • Validation to protect inputs

This is exactly what real apps like Instagram, Facebook, and Netflix use.

⚡ Developer Mindset

👉 This is not just HTML anymore — this is UI engineering.

syntax_breakdown.content:
🧪 Login Form Structure Example

<form>
  <h2>Login</h2>

  <label for="email">Email</label>
  <input id="email" type="email" required>

  <label for="password">Password</label>
  <input id="password" type="password" required minlength="6">

  <button>Login</button>
</form>

🧪 Signup Form Structure Example

<form>
  <h2>Sign Up</h2>

  <label>Username</label>
  <input type="text" required>

  <label>Email</label>
  <input type="email" required>

  <label>Password</label>
  <input type="password" required minlength="6">

  <button>Create Account</button>
</form>

🧠 Key Idea

👉 Real apps are just structured HTML forms + logic later (JavaScript + Firebase)

editor_sandbox.starter_code:
Welcome to Cymor App

Create or login to your account

mini_challenge.instruction:
🎯 FINAL MISSION: Build a working login + signup UI

You must include:

1. A LOGIN form
   - email input
   - password input
   - validation (required + minlength 6)

2. A SIGNUP form
   - username input
   - email input
   - password input
   - validation on all fields

3. Proper labels for all inputs
4. Proper HTML structure

🔥 This is your first real application UI
mini_challenge.hint:
👉 Structure:
<form>Login...</form>
<form>Signup...</form>

Use labels + inputs + buttons inside each form

quiz_engine.question: What is the main purpose of this login/signup project?
options: To build a real-world user authentication interface layout
quiz_engine.explanation_feedback: Correct 🎉 This project simulates real-world authentication interfaces used in modern web applications.

takeaways: Login pages are built using HTML forms
Signup pages collect user registration data
Labels and validation make forms professional
Real apps combine forms + backend systems later
You just built your first real UI system
